refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined productivity target by optimizing equipment operation and task execution efficiency.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and outcome-focused; refining it into sub-FRs clarifies specific functional aspects that contribute to productivity, enabling targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure actual productivity (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations and compare against the customer-defined target; verify sub-FRs via operational metrics and system logs.
sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operational parameters to maximize throughput.
FR2: Efficiently schedule and execute worksite tasks to minimize idle time.
FR3: Provide real-time performance feedback to operators and tele-operators.

candidate_dps: DP1: Adaptive equipment control algorithms to optimize operational parameters.
DP2: Dynamic task scheduling and dispatch system.
DP3: Real-time telemetry and feedback interface.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Adaptive equipment control algorithms to optimize operational parameters.

potential_sources: Interaction between control algorithms and task scheduling
coupling_check.notes: DP1 is logically separable but requires interfaces to DP2 and DP3 for data and command exchange; coupling is manageable with well-defined interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate sensor data for control algorithms.
Risk of suboptimal performance if task scheduling is not well integrated.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent decomposition levels to address coupling.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical control and operational parameter optimization of the heavy equipment.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exchange of sensor data and operational status between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receive scheduled tasks and commands from Teleoperation or NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as control algorithms directly operate on vehicle hardware; low diffusion risk.
Moderate containment; some control logic could be centralized here but less direct hardware access.
Low containment; primarily for remote control and tasking, not direct control algorithms.
Minimal relevance to productivity control algorithms.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Implementing adaptive control algorithms within the Vehicle module ensures tight coupling with hardware and minimizes interface complexity.
containment_risks: Potential difficulty in updating control algorithms if tightly embedded in Vehicle hardware.
Risk of insufficient integration with task scheduling if interfaces are not well defined.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules to mitigate integration risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR by providing adaptive control to optimize productivity.

diffusion_risks: Some control logic diffusion risk if parts of the algorithm are implemented outside Vehicle module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment for DP; interfaces are well identified to manage coupling.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring productivity metrics and monitoring telemetry data from Vehicle module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of control function to Vehicle module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Further refine interfaces with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution to ensure seamless tasking and telemetry integration.
Plan for modular updates to control algorithms within Vehicle to address potential update risks.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, the coupling with task scheduling and telemetry modules requires clearer interface definitions and risk mitigation.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operational parameters to maximize throughput.
FR2: Efficiently schedule and execute worksite tasks to minimize idle time.
FR3: Provide real-time performance feedback to operators and tele-operators.

revise.why: To better define and formalize interfaces and ownership boundaries with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules to reduce diffusion risks.

assumptions: Sensor data is reliable and timely.
Task scheduling and telemetry systems will be developed in parallel.
open_risks: Integration complexity between Vehicle control and task scheduling.
Update and maintenance challenges for embedded control algorithms.
evidence_needed: Performance data from prototype Vehicle control algorithms.
Interface specifications and data exchange protocols with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution.
